context: User is on branch perdix-140-add-leaderboard with an open PR #42 that has 4 unresolved review threads: a must-fix on lib/db/queries.ts, a nit suggestion in components/game/leaderboard-card.tsx, a second suggestion, and one question. gh CLI is authenticated.
expected_behavior: Step 1 detects the PR for the current branch via `gh pr view --json number,headRefName,url,state,reviewDecision,title`
Step 2 fetches review threads via the gh api graphql query on reviewThreads(first: 100) to get isResolved, path, line, comments with author/body
Since 4 unresolved threads exist, skips Step 3 (AI discovery) and goes directly to Step 4

Step 4 spawns ALL 4 feature-dev:code-reviewer subagents in a single tool-call message (parallel, no context bleed) — each with the per-thread prompt including PR title/description, comment, file path/line, categorization instructions, and the 'Produce Hard Evidence' reproduction-first block

For the must-fix thread, the subagent runs the reproduction loop: writes a minimal failing test beside lib/db/queries.ts, runs it against current code and captures RED_OUTPUT (must fail), applies the fix, re-runs and captures GREEN_OUTPUT (must pass), then reverts the fix AND deletes the repro test and confirms `git diff --stat` is empty

Each subagent returns the STRICT format including EVIDENCE_TIER, REPRO_TEST, REPRO_COMMANDS, RED_OUTPUT, GREEN_OUTPUT — the must-fix at EVIDENCE_TIER: reproduced, the style nit (no bug claim) at fix-validated, the question at n/a

Step 5 aggregates, drops approvals, splits findings into Confirmed (reproduced/tooling-confirmed) vs 'Unconfirmed — reasoning only', sorts must-fix > suggestion > question, and renders the summary table with an Evidence column (✓ reproduced / ◆ tooling-confirmed / ⚙ fix-validated / ✗ unverified)

Any reply drafted for the question thread (RESPONSE_DRAFT) is in Conventional Comments format ('<label> [decoration]: <subject>', e.g. 'note (non-blocking): ...'), per practices/pr-review.md — not bare prose
Any commit peep makes to push the fixes uses a Conventional Commits message
Before committing, Step 10 runs the full CI-equivalent suite (typecheck && lint && test) even though each fix individually passed
failure_modes: Posts a bare-prose reply to the question thread instead of a Conventional Comments-formatted one
Spawns subagents sequentially (one at a time, awaiting each) instead of in a single parallel tool-call message
Analyzes all 4 comments in a single shared context, allowing findings from one thread to bias another
Reports the must-fix as confirmed on the strength of 'tsc + tests pass' without ever writing a test that FAILS on current code — i.e. proves the fix builds but not that the bug was real
Leaves the repro test file behind after validation instead of deleting it, leaving the working tree dirty
Skips the full-suite validation in Step 10 because per-fix validation already passed, missing cross-file type conflicts
